description:
You are acting as a junior backup-integrity engineer who has just logged in to a fresh Linux workstation
    (your home directory is /home/user).  
    A senior colleague has asked you to validate and fix a couple of symbolic links that are critical for the
    nightly backup procedure.

    Current situation (already present in the filesystem):
    1. /home/user/data/live/important.db                        – real SQLite file (already present, untouched).
    2. /home/user/archives/snap_latest                          – symbolic link, but it is presently broken
       because it points to /home/user/data/old_live (which no longer exists).

    Your tasks (perform them exactly in this order – it should take only three or four terminal commands):
    A. Repair /home/user/archives/snap_latest so that it becomes a symbolic link that resolves to
       the directory  /home/user/data/live  (nothing else; make sure it is not a hard link).
    B. Create the directory  /home/user/backup_review  if it does not already exist.
    C. Inside /home/user/backup_review, create a symbolic link called  latest_db
       that points to  /home/user/data/live/important.db .
    D. Produce a plain-text validation log called
       /home/user/backup_review/integrity_symlinks.log
       containing EXACTLY the two lines shown below (Unix line endings, **each line terminated by
       a single LF**, no extra blank lines, no trailing spaces):

       /home/user/archives/snap_latest -> /home/user/data/live
       /home/user/backup_review/latest_db -> /home/user/data/live/important.db

    IMPORTANT – The automated grader will:
    • Confirm that /home/user/archives/snap_latest is a symbolic link resolving exactly to /home/user/data/live.
    • Confirm that /home/user/backup_review/latest_db is a symbolic link resolving exactly to /home/user/data/live/important.db.
    • Read /home/user/backup_review/integrity_symlinks.log and expect precisely the
      two lines above (including the final newline after the second line).

    Do NOT add any additional text to integrity_symlinks.log.
    You do not need root privileges; everything should be doable as the normal user.
truth:
<!-- Pre-existing filesystem objects (before the agent begins) -->
    Directory:  /home/user/data/live
    File:       /home/user/data/live/important.db
                Contents (binary/irrelevant for the task – integrity checking is only about the link itself).

    Symbolic link (broken):  /home/user/archives/snap_latest  ->  /home/user/data/old_live

    <!-- Required end state after task completion -->
    Symbolic link: /home/user/archives/snap_latest          -> /home/user/data/live
    Symbolic link: /home/user/backup_review/latest_db       -> /home/user/data/live/important.db
    File:          /home/user/backup_review/integrity_symlinks.log
    Exact contents (including final LF):
        /home/user/archives/snap_latest -> /home/user/data/live\n
        /home/user/backup_review/latest_db -> /home/user/data/live/important.db\n
name: task_000000_77f5267e
